item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's nuts. I would never have guessed that the similarities between this and the class were coincidental.
I mean, so many of the thematic elements are there: high school, water, loss of memory, reliving the past. Just one of those crazy coincidences, I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Admittedly, I didn't write this with the Class of 76 in mind, as I'm not too familiar with its canon. Reading through some of the other tagged pages, though, I can see how you could make the connection.

Thanks for the comment!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. I don't have much to say on that front, other than that the Temperance-Principality event is a clever plot device to explain the Foundation letting this stuff happen.

What I do want to say is, it's pretty clear that this is a Class of 76 anomaly, so it should probably have the tag.
The way it plays with timelines is really interesting. If I had to complain about anything in this article, it'll be of how many footnotes it has.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same. I get that it's meant to be a context reference that doesn't get explained fully, but the number of times it gets hammered on in such a relatively short article makes it feel like "TEMPERANCE PRINCIPALITY EVENT" is written in neon in the sky and what we should be focused on rather than the characters or events around the SCP. Additionally, the guy who got away by using the something-item is also a weird side-reference that kind of pulls attention away from the idea.

If these are things that get expanded on elsewhere, this article feels a bit jarring without more direct reference to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The references to the "TEMPERANCE-PRINCIPALITY event"1 are a bit too distracting to my tastes to fully give this an upvote.

Footnotes
1. I'm not sure if it's referencing anything on the site currently.

item.title: Never Miss the Last Day of Summer
item.content:encoded:
This is an SCP about a place that's very important to me.

Special thanks to Zyn and caspian2 for critique. Image was taken by me.
